content.claim: Per-capita supplementary grants for SNI students constitute only a weak financial incentive to reduce learning disadvantage in the absence of strict requirements tied to local educational programs, and foundation schools may use SNI status as a funding consideration despite reversed assessment-admission sequencing.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: The claim about weak top-up incentives is grounded in general public-finance theory rather than a dedicated Hungarian case audit
The foundation-school financing mechanism claim is corroborated by only a single practitioner-facing guidance source, not by audited enrollment or funding data
No quantified case count of reclassification-seeking driven by funding at foundation schools was found
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
